data.title: DRI Healthcare Trust appoints Zaheed Mawani as CFO
data.description:
• DRI Healthcare Trust (TSX:DHT.U:CA) on Friday announced the promotion of Zaheed Mawani to chief financial officer, effective October 1, 2025.
  • Mawani joined DRI Healthcare in October 2024 as Chief Compliance Officer. In his new role as CFO, Mawani will continue to oversee these responsibilities while assuming the leadership of DRI Healthcare's financial and strategic planning.
  • Prior to joining DRI Healthcare, Mawani held multiple senior roles at large multi-national organizations, including senior finance leadership roles at Ritchie Bros.
  • The company said Amit Kapur, the outgoing CFO, will continue to support DRI Healthcare through September 30, 2025, to ensure a smooth transition of responsibilities.
  • Source: press release

data.title: Trump considering website for consumers to buy discounted prescription drugs - report
data.description:
• The Trump administration is said to be discussing the launch of a website that would allow consumers to purchase prescription drugs less expensively by buying them directly from pharmaceutical companies.
  • White House officials are also considering naming the website "TrumpRx," Bloomberg reported.
  • The development comes as a Sept. 29 deadline for drug companies to lower prices set by the administration's "most favored nation" drug pricing policy is quickly approaching. 
  • As envisioned, patients would be able to look up drugs on the website and then be provided with links to platforms that sell them, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Genuine Parts considers separation of auto and industrial businesses
data.description:
Shares of Genuine Parts Company (NYSE:GPC) gained ground in late trading on Friday following a report that the company was considering separating its industrial parts business from its auto parts business.

According to sources cited by Bloomberg, talks are reportedly at an early stage and could result in the company deciding to keep the current corporate structure intact.

Genuine Parts (NYSE:GPC) recently settled with Elliott Investment Management to appoint two Elliott-endorsed members to the board, replacing retiring members Robert Loudermilk, Jr., and John Holder. The board appointments were in connection with a Cooperation Agreement between GPC and Elliott, leading to speculation that Elliott would pressure the company to split into two parts.

At the time, Elliott’s Marc Steinberg suggested that a separation of the business would unlock the value of its automotive and industrial businesses.

“We believe the company's current share price does not reflect the true value of its automotive aftermarket and industrial distribution businesses, and that there is a clear path to creating substantial, long-term value at GPC,” Steinberg said.

The automotive business generates roughly two-thirds of the company’s revenue while industrial makes up the remaining third.

data.title: AAM announces pricing and upsizing of senior secured and unsecured notes
data.description:
• American Axle & Manufacturing's (NYSE:AXL) wholly-owned subsidiary American Axle & Manufacturing has priced its previously announced offering of $850 million of 6.375% senior secured notes due 2032 and $1.25 billion of 7.750% senior unsecured notes due 2033.
  • The issuer intends to use the net proceeds from this offering, together with borrowings under its existing credit agreement and cash on hand to pay the cash consideration payable in connection with the pending business combination with Dowlais Group, to repay in full all outstanding borrowings under the existing credit facilities, to fund a change of control offer for certain outstanding notes of Dowlais, to fund the redemption of all of the Issuer's 6.50% Notes due 2027 and for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Boeing nears 250-jet order from Turkey as Trump to meet Erdogan - Bloomberg
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) is poised to win an order from Turkey as soon as next week for as many as 250 commercial planes and additional Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) F-16 fighter jets, with a possible resolution of a long-running F-35 dispute, President Trump said Friday in a social media post as he plans to host Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan in Washington on September 25.

"We are working on many Trade and Military Deals with the President, including the large scale purchase of Boeing aircraft, a major F-16 Deal, and a continuation of the F-35 talks, which we expect to conclude positively," Trump posted.

Boeing (NYSE:BA) has hammered out the framework for a deal with Turkish Airlines, which would be the culmination of years of negotiations, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: Datavault AI registers 5.3M shares for resale tied to CompuSystems asset purchase
data.description:
• Datavault AI (NASDAQ:DVLT) filed a prospectus for the resale of up to 5.3M shares of restricted common stock.
  • The shares were issued on May 20, 2025, in connection with the acquisition of assets from CompuSystems.
  • The issuance was governed by an Asset Purchase Agreement dated December 19, 2024, and amended four times through May 14, 2025.
  • DVLT shares 5.8% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: SEC filing
